summary: `temper why`'s edge walk must range over the gate's resolved edge set (relationships over extracted features), not the private `[edge.*]` document-clause re-derivation

per.section: Decision: the CLI gains a read family â€” `why` and `requirements`

edit.description: Expose the resolved-edge computation so the read family consumes the exact same arcs the gate does â€” never a private re-derivation. Make `resolved_arcs` (or a `(kind,id)`-node in/out enumeration built from it) `pub(crate)`, keyed `(kind, id)` as today, so `read::why` can look up a member node's incoming/outgoing resolved edges through the one function `graph::check`/`acyclic`/`degree` already range over.

Replace the `EdgeClause`-based edge-in/edge-out traversal (the `Member.edges` field sourced from `skill.edges`/`rule.edges`) with the graph's resolved edge set: `why` takes the same `by_kind` features + `edges` (or the resolved arcs) the check arm builds and narrates the incoming/outgoing edges of the member's `(kind, id)` node from that, so its edge narration cannot disagree with `graph::check`. The `satisfies`/roster walk (the requirementâ†”`satisfies` edge) is unchanged. Drop the now-unused `EdgeClause` edge import from the read view.

The `Command::Why` arm builds nothing today; make it construct the same `by_kind` Features map + declared `edges` (built-in `layer.edges()` plus each registered custom kind's `[[relationships]]`) the `check` arm builds (main.rs ~634-668), and pass them to `read::why`. Extract that by_kind/edges construction into a shared helper so the read arm and the check arm derive one identical edge set by construction (the inbox's 'one source of truth'). `Command::Requirements` is unaffected (it walks only `satisfies`/roster).

Rework the edge fixtures + snapshots: author edges via a `temper.toml` `[[kind.rule.relationships]]` declaration (`field = routes_to`, `to = skill`) plus an extracted `routes_to` field on the source member â€” not `[edge.<target>]` clauses via `skill.edges` â€” and re-record the `why_dev_standards`/`why_lint_runner` insta snapshots. Assert `why <rule>` shows the outgoing edge and `why <skill>` the incoming one over the same edges `graph::check` resolves, and that a member with no declared edge stays silent.

tests.asserts: `why` over a `routes_to`-connected ruleâ†’skill pair â€” declared as a `[[kind.relationships]]` edge over an extracted field, with no `[edge.*]` clause â€” surfaces the outgoing edge on the rule and the incoming edge on the skill (the exact set `graph::check` resolves), and stays silent when no edge is declared.

acceptance: `temper why <member>` narrates the incoming/outgoing edges of the exact resolved edge set `graph::check`/`acyclic`/`degree` range over (relationship declarations over extracted features, incl. the `extra` `routes_to` field and custom-kind relationships) â€” no divergence between the read family and the gate; both derive the edge set from one shared construction; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Dogfood catch (2nd harness): `why` reads `[edge.*]` document clauses while `graph::check` reads relationships over extracted features â€” they disagree on a `routes_to` edge. Fix per inbox: the read family consumes the gate's resolved set, one source of truth, no private re-derivation. Whether `[edge.*]` should itself feed the graph is a separate fork â€” see `(edge-representation-unify)`; this entry builds on the gate's edge set as-is, so it needs no fork resolution.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind â€” `.claude/agents/*.md` imported as one AGENT.md member document, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the requirements/satisfies reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; the whole surface-language chain it waited on has shipped, so the blocker is priority alone â€” revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The Agent IR â€” the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs`: typed YAML frontmatter on the projection side (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`), unknown keys preserved, content-faithful body, provenance. Identity is the path (file stem). The surface member is one AGENT.md document (clause-module header) like every kind.

The agent kind's authored built-in package (clauses + guidance) in the one-document medium, embedded with the std-lib (the EMBED-BUILTIN-PACKAGES model â€” NOT a contracts/agent.toml). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*` and a `WorkspaceError` arm; update the load tests.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to one `<into>/agents/<name>/AGENT.md` member document, add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent.
Bind the agent package, wire admissibility + conformance streams and `by_kind` for the kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Shaped to the surface-language forms â€” one AGENT.md member document, an authored PACKAGE.md, no meta.toml, no contracts/agent.toml. On revival its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes via blockedBy against any then-open entry touching those files (e.g. READ-EDGE-UNIFY, which edits main.rs).

summary: Ship acquisition channels â€” npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install â€” auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest â€” reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up.
